title: Subtracting by Jumping Back on a Number Line

steps.work: Imagine a number line from 0 to 10. Start at 9.
steps.explanation: We start at the bigger number and jump back.
Each jump is one space to the left. We make 4 jumps total.

steps.instruction: What number did you land on?
steps.explanation: The number you land on after jumping back is the answer.

title: Using a Friendly Number on the Number Line

steps.instruction: Find 13 − 7 on a number line. Use 10 as a stopping point.
steps.work: Start at 13. Instead of 7 small jumps, break it into two jumps through 10.
steps.explanation: 10 is a friendly number — it makes subtraction easier.

steps.instruction: Jump from 13 back to 10. How many is that?
steps.work: 13 → 10 is 3 jumps back. We still need to jump 7 − 3 = 4 more.
steps.explanation: We used 3 of our 7 jumps to reach 10.
Breaking the jump at 10 turned one hard subtraction into two easy ones: 13 − 3 = 10, then 10 − 4 = 6.
